summary: Backend-only retest of Global Hanta Map. Re-ran the full pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py against the live backend via REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. All 30 tests passed in 4.37s covering health, config, outbreaks (list/filter severity/search/country detail/404), global stats, global timeline, news + breaking news, public ad-slots, subscription upsert, AI summary (Claude Sonnet 4.5 via Emergent LLM key), auth (OAuth2 form + JSON + invalid + /me + 401), and admin CRUD (outbreaks PATCH with fatality_rate auto-recalc + 404 + empty 400, news create/list/delete/double-delete 404, subscriptions list, ad-slots list+PATCH, analytics with 30-day revenue_series, refresh-now scheduling).
critical_code_review_comments: Mocked/stub items (already disclosed by main agent): admin/analytics revenue_series is simulated; scheduler refresh job only updates last_refresh_check timestamp (no real WHO/CDC/PAHO ingestion); outbreak seed data is illustrative only; email/Telegram/Twitter sending not implemented. These are clearly flagged in code comments and the review request.
server.py mixes models, lifespan, public/auth/admin routes (~500 LOC). Recommend splitting into routers/{public,auth,admin}.py and models.py before adding more features.
auth.py JWT_SECRET has a 'change-me'-style fallback - acceptable for MVP but should fail-fast on missing env in production.
AI summary endpoint /api/ai/summary has no rate limiting - public + LLM-billed endpoint. Add per-IP throttling before public launch to prevent abuse.
PATCH /api/admin/outbreaks/{cc} does not append updated counts into the timelines collection, so the historical chart will not reflect admin edits. Acceptable MVP limitation, document for users.

Admin PATCH on Argentina from prior iteration left confirmed_cases=999, deaths=50 in the DB (test asserts persisted state). Tests are stable across runs because they overwrite to known values, but seed data is not reset between iterations - reseed/reset DB if deterministic dashboard totals are required.

Subscription endpoint upsert is correct, but on re-subscribe with same email the response carries the new countries while id is regenerated only for legacy docs missing one - minor cosmetic inconsistency.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created in this iteration. Backend auto-seeds 16 countries + timelines + 15 news + admin user + 5 ad-slots on first startup via lifespan hook. Admin PATCH from iteration_1 still leaves AR.confirmed_cases=999 in DB (test re-asserts and overwrites to 999/50 each run).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is the canonical regression set (30 tests, ~4.4s). Run: `python -m pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --tb=short --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. All endpoints from the review request are covered including mocked/stub endpoints (analytics revenue_series, refresh-now scheduler stub). Tests authenticate using OAuth2 form login first then fall back to JSON login. Argentina (AR) outbreak is the canonical fixture used for admin PATCH and AI summary tests.

tests_executed.backend_pytest: 30/30 passed - TestPublic (12), TestSubscription (1), TestAI (2), TestAuth (5), TestAdmin (10)
tests_executed.frontend_playwright: skipped per main agent request (backend-only iteration)
tests_executed.integration_verified: Admin PATCH on AR persists and reflects in public GET /api/outbreaks/AR (confirmed_cases=999, deaths=50, fatality_rate auto-recomputed); admin news create -> list -> delete -> double-delete 404 round-trip; OAuth2 form + JSON login both return identical access_token shape and pass /api/auth/me as role=admin.

rca of the issue: No issues encountered - all 30 backend tests pass on first run.
